title: realloc Semantics and Transactional Growth · Changed-Lifetime Lab
summary: An independent maintainer ports the amortized vector with failure invariants to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of amortized vector with failure invariants with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Store realloc results transactionally so allocation failure preserves the original live block.
Invalidate or rebase all interior pointers and aliases when realloc may move storage.
Choose bounded geometric growth from amortized cost, overflow, maximum capacity, and memory-pressure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Realloc Temporary Pointer
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 2412. Produce bounded C evidence to grow capacity without losing the old allocation on failure; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
